---
title: VA Veteran Status Attribute Service — Adapter Technical Specification
doc-type: ats
adapter-id: va-veteran-attribute-service
adapter-class: conformance
mission-class: identity
status: reserved
phase: phase-planning
gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate
canon-source: src/uiao/canon/adapter-registry.yaml
derived-from: uiao/canon (sync_canon.py)
generated: '2026-05-07'
---

# VA Veteran Status Attribute Service — Adapter Technical Specification

::: {.callout-note}
## Canon-derived document

**Status:** `reserved` · **Class:** `conformance` · **Mission:** `identity` · **Phase:** `phase-planning`

**Canon source:** `canon/adapter-registry.yaml` (propagated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`).

The YAML frontmatter and this banner are regenerated from canon on every sync. **Do not hand-edit.** Author new material only below the `## Overview` heading.
:::

## Overview

The VA Veteran Status Attribute Service Adapter is the **veteran-status attribute observer** of the UIAO substrate. As a conformance-class identity adapter, it observes veteran-status disclosure events from the Department of Veterans Affairs (in coordination with DoD) — the federal authority of record for veteran status, service record, dependent status, and veteran preference per **5 U.S.C. §2108** — and emits canonical claims with the disclosure-audit envelope.

The veteran-status attribute is a long-retention surface subject to **USERRA**, the **Privacy Act**, and **veteran-preference statutes**. Consuming engagements include federal-employment veteran-preference adjudication, VA-benefit eligibility decisions, and dependent-benefit determinations.

Three primary functions:

1. **Veteran-status disclosure observation.** Watches veteran-status disclosures from VA's authoritative channels — verified veteran flag, service-period details (where authorized), discharge characterization (where authorized), service-connected disability status (where authorized) — emitting canonical claims with full provenance.
2. **Veteran-preference attestation.** Records the canonical veteran-preference category (5-point, 10-point, derived preference, etc.) for federal-employment decision points, citing the 5 U.S.C. §2108 basis.
3. **Dependent-status observation.** Captures dependent-status disclosures (spouse, dependent child) for benefit-eligibility flows that require the attribute, with full authority citation.

**Implementation:** Reserved slot per ADR-049. Activation requires a per-adapter ADR modeled on ADR-035.

## Scope

Target surfaces / subsystems: `veteran-status`, `service-record`, `dependent-status`, `veteran-preference`

**Reads:** VA authoritative channels (in coordination with DoD authoritative records) for veteran status, service record, dependent status, and veteran preference. Access gated by USERRA, the Privacy Act, and the applicable veteran-preference statute; entitlement recorded in `canon/reciprocal-consumption-registry.yaml` with the statutory citation.

**Emits:** `va-attribute-response.json` (per-disclosure canonical claim with verified-veteran flag, preference category, service-period summary where authorized), `va-disclosure-audit.json` (per-disclosure audit record with statutory citation, entitlement reference, VA disclosure id), plus `EvidenceObject` records that feed the IA-2 / IA-4 / AU-2 control evidence stream.

**Does NOT:** issue or modify VA records (VA / DoD are the authorities of record), access medical or claims-detail records, modify veteran-preference determinations, or store service-record detail beyond what the disclosure surfaces.

## Controls

NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 controls this adapter supports: `IA-2`, `IA-4`, `AU-2`, `AU-12`, `SC-8`, `SC-13`

| Control | Role | Adapter capability |
|---|---|---|
| **IA-2** Identification and Authentication | Supporting | Veteran-status claims supplement identification when veteran-preference or VA-benefit eligibility is required. |
| **IA-4** Identifier Management | Primary | VA disclosure id is the stable identifier per disclosure event. |
| **AU-2** Event Logging | Primary | Every disclosure is logged with the canonical disclosure-audit envelope, statutory citation, entitlement reference. |
| **AU-12** Audit Record Generation | Supporting | Audit records carry the canonical provenance envelope per ADR-006. |
| **SC-8** Transmission Confidentiality and Integrity | Primary | All VA channels are TLS-protected. |
| **SC-13** Cryptographic Protection | Supporting | Validation uses FIPS-validated cryptographic libraries. |

## Operational profile

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Runtime | `TBD` |
| Runtime pin | `TBD` |
| Runner class | `TBD` |
| Tenancy | `per-customer` |
| Evidence class | `baseline` |
| Retention | `3` year(s) |

## Canon invariants

- `gcc-boundary: gcc-moderate`
- `ssot-mutation: never`
- `certificate-anchored: true`
- `object-identity-only: true`

## Notes from canon

VA (in coordination with DoD) is the federal authority of record for veteran status, service record, dependent status, and veteran preference (5 U.S.C. §2108). Long-retention attribute surface; subject to USERRA, Privacy Act, and veteran-preference statutes. Activation requires a per-adapter ADR modeled on ADR-035.

Member of the federal attribute-service family.

ZTMM pillars: identity, data.

## References

- UIAO-CANON-003
- ADR-049 — Reserved-slot allocation framework

---

*Generated by `uiao/tools/sync_canon.py`. See `uiao/ARCHITECTURE.md` §4 for the cross-repo sync contract. See `uiao-docs/_quarto.yml` for rendering configuration.*
